level_signals: Comp range $232K–$290K base consistent with Senior IC
Expected to own roadmap for a major product area end-to-end

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Have 6+ years of relevant experience (e.g., product manager, founder, engineer, researcher etc.)
Strong technical acumen — comfortable discussing APIs, data models, and architecture with engineers.
Proven track record of shipping impactful features in fast-paced environments.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: A Computer Science, Engineering, or Math degree or equivalent technical experience.
Previous startup or high-growth company experience.

scope.surface: Decagon's core platform — agent building, testing, experimentation, analytics, QA, versioning, integrations, and enterprise controls
scope.business_model: B2B SaaS, conversational AI platform for enterprise customers
primary_metrics_inferred: Speed and quality of agent configuration and deployment
core_responsibilities: Shape the roadmap for core capabilities that power the next generation of AI agents from first concept to launch
Work closely with customers to uncover new ways AI can transform their workflows, unlock automation, and elevate customer experience
Translate breakthrough ideas into clear product specs, then partner with engineering and design to bring them to life
Own initiatives end-to-end, from discovery and scoping to launch and adoption

green_flags: Compensation range disclosed and competitive ($232K–$290K base plus equity)
Founding PM role with high ownership and influence over core platform
Backed by top-tier investors (a16z, Accel, Bain Capital Ventures, Coatue, Index Ventures)
Clear product surface and team mission described in detail
Broad definition of relevant experience (PM, founder, engineer, researcher)
red_flags: Explicitly in-office company; no remote option stated — eliminates distributed candidates
Office city/location not specified in the JD — candidates cannot assess commute viability
Visa sponsorship and work authorization not addressed
No mention of team size or direct reports structure

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from comp range + founding PM framing + years requirement)
scope.business_model (inferred from product description and customer segments)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not stated in JD)
location.cities_listed (no specific office city named in the JD body)
location.remote_us_eligible (inferred from 'We're an in-office company' statement)
ambiguities: JD states 'We're an in-office company' but does not specify which city or cities the office is in — location.cities_listed left empty
Work authorization and visa sponsorship are not mentioned anywhere in the JD — left as 'unclear'
Compensation listed as '$232K – $290K + Offers Equity' — unclear whether this is base or total cash; treated as base given standard JD conventions
